audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for spiritual gifts, one Spirit/many gifts, one body/many members, weaker members honored, gifts ranked toward love in chapter 13.

verses.restored: Now concerning spiritual things/gifts, siblings, I do not want you to be unaware.
You know that when you were nations, you were being led away to voiceless idols, however you were led.
Therefore I make known to you that no one speaking in Spirit of the Divine says, Jesus is accursed; and no one is able to say, Jesus is Lord, except in holy Spirit.
There are varieties of grace-gifts, but the same Spirit;
and there are varieties of services, but the same Lord;
and there are varieties of workings, but the same Divine, the One working all things in all.
To each is given the manifestation of the Spirit toward what benefits.
For to one through the Spirit is given word of wisdom, and to another word of knowledge according to the same Spirit,
to another trust in the same Spirit, to another grace-gifts of healings in the one Spirit,
to another workings of powers, to another prophecy, to another discernings of spirits, to another kinds of tongues, to another interpretation of tongues.
But all these things the one and same Spirit works, distributing to each individually just as he wills.
For just as the body is one and has many members, and all the members of the body, being many, are one body, so also Messiah.
For in one Spirit we all were baptized into one body, whether Judeans or Greeks, whether enslaved or free, and all were given one Spirit to drink.
For the body also is not one member but many.
If the foot should say, Because I am not a hand, I am not from the body, not because of this is it not from the body.
And if the ear should say, Because I am not an eye, I am not from the body, not because of this is it not from the body.
If the whole body were eye, where would hearing be? If the whole were hearing, where would smell be?
But now the Divine placed the members, each one of them, in the body just as he willed.
If all were one member, where would the body be?
But now many members, yet one body.
The eye is not able to say to the hand, I have no need of you; or again the head to the feet, I have no need of you.
But much more, the members of the body that seem to be weaker are necessary,
and those we think to be less honorable in the body, these we clothe with more abundant honor; and our unpresentable parts have more abundant presentability,
but our presentable parts have no need. But the Divine blended the body together, giving more abundant honor to the one lacking,
so that there may be no split in the body, but that the members may have the same care for one another.
And if one member suffers, all the members suffer together; if one member is glorified, all the members rejoice together.
Now you are body of Messiah and members from a part.
And the Divine placed some in the assembly: first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then powers, then grace-gifts of healings, helps, guidances, kinds of tongues.
Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Are all powers?
Do all have grace-gifts of healings? Do all speak in tongues? Do all interpret?
But desire the greater grace-gifts. And still I show you a way beyond measure.

translation_notes: Pneumatika in verse 1 may mean spiritual things or spiritual gifts; restored wording keeps both.
The body section is anti-hierarchy medicine: weaker-seeming members are necessary and receive more abundant honor.
